sections.heading: Global Marketing Strategy 2025
sections.content:
Global Marketing Strategy 2025
Building Brand Presence Across Emerging and Established Markets

Q1 Strategic Review

[Speaker Notes]
Welcome the team. This deck covers our full-year strategy refresh and the three growth pillars we are committing to in 2025.

sections.heading: Market Landscape
sections.content:
Market Landscape
Key Observations:
• Consumer spending in emerging markets grew 14% YoY
• Digital advertising now exceeds traditional channels in 38 countries
• Brand loyalty is declining — experience now drives 65% of purchase decisions
• Sustainability credentials are a top-3 factor for under-35 consumers globally

[Speaker Notes]
Stress the shift in consumer values. The data on brand loyalty is particularly striking and justifies our experience-first pivot.

sections.heading: Three Growth Pillars
sections.content:
Three Growth Pillars
Experience-Led Campaigns — immersive activations that create shareable moments
Localised Content at Scale — culturally resonant messaging in 12 new markets
Sustainability Storytelling — authentic, measurable impact narratives

[Speaker Notes]
Each pillar has a dedicated budget allocation and a named owner. We will review pillar performance quarterly.

sections.heading: 2025 Budget Allocation
sections.content:
2025 Budget Allocation
Total Budget: $48M across all regions
• Digital & Social: 42%
• Experiential Events: 23%
• Content Production: 18%
• Partnerships & Sponsorships: 11%
• Research & Analytics: 6%

[Speaker Notes]
The increase in experiential spend reflects pillar one. The analytics investment is essential for proving ROI across all channels.

sections.heading: Key Performance Indicators
sections.content:
Key Performance Indicators
We will track success against the following targets:
• Brand Awareness: +12 points in 5 priority markets
• Net Promoter Score: from 34 to 45 globally
• Digital Engagement Rate: 4.5% average across owned channels
• Campaign Attribution Revenue: $120M influenced pipeline

[Speaker Notes]
These are stretch targets. Emphasise that the KPIs were set collaboratively with regional leads, not imposed top-down.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.content:
Next Steps
Immediate Actions:
• Finalise regional market plans by end of Q1
• Brief creative agencies on experience-led campaign briefs
• Launch sustainability audit across all product lines
• Schedule quarterly business review cadence

[Speaker Notes]
Assign owners before we close the session. Each action item has a named DRI and a deadline in the project tracker.
